decision.default_long_context_representation: task-specific: boundary-neighbor blocks for cleanup, current-only for chunk summaries, full transcript only for direct whole summary
decision.conditional_structured_representation: compact generic JSON blocks with application-owned identity and timestamp reattachment

safe_concurrency.bounded_short_context_selected_contracts: P2/P4 structural and semantic screening passed; matched repeated production qualification remains open

E2B.position: speed-first conservative copier or bounded JSON helper
proven_facts: Long plain input at about 23.4k prompt tokens completed and preserved the current chunk, but cleanup gain was weak.
The long compact schema-output arm failed 2/2 with HTTP 200, finish=length, 1620/1620 completion tokens, and 1227 reasoning tokens; visible JSON was partial and unparseable.
The failure is a reasoning/output-budget interaction under the tested contract, not a general large-context collapse.
Bounded M01/M05 raw JSON and repaired generic-schema P4 remain strengths.
E2B.recommendation: Do not use the tested 23k compact schema-output contract. Require semantic completeness and cleanup-gain validation.
unresolved: No independently verified zero-reasoning or larger-budget decomposition for the long schema-output arm.

E4B.position: bounded plain-input option with task-specific blocks
proven_facts: One long same-prompt compact schema-output arm completed with stop, zero reasoning, parseable raw JSON, exact narrow schema, and complete current-chunk content.
This is one syntactic and semantic feasibility cell, not repeatability or admission.
Separate JSON/timestamped input-representation evidence retained a silent early-tail deletion.
M05 malformed runaway remains deterministic through 4096, 8192, and 16384 output-token caps.
E4B.recommendation: Keep plain input as default. Use schema-output only with semantic and completeness validation. Block M05-like repeated-tail shapes.

proven_facts: Plain closure outputs were complete and chunk-only with 13/13 exact protected numeric values; the ordered merge was complete.
Block closure outputs mechanically merged 24/24 exact IDs in order with no missing, duplicate, or extra IDs and retained semantic content.
Initial full-prefix P2 attempts failed 0/4 before generation; all positions succeeded sequentially.
Individual about-23k requests fit the tested 32k runtime, but two such requests did not fit concurrently.
Bounded 8k P2 and repaired generic-schema P4 results do not admit full-prefix P2/P4.
12B QAT.recommendation: Use plain input and P1 sequential for the tested full-prefix workload. Use compact blocks when identity and mechanical merge matter. Treat output as a reviewable draft.
unresolved: No second recording or exhaustive source-unit alignment.
No active-application persistence read-back or original-block fallback merge.

26B MoE.position: high-latency option requiring workload-specific quality proof
proven_facts: No stable quality advantage over 12B was demonstrated.
Bounded P2 and repaired generic-schema P4 passed structurally, but latency was much higher.
Controlled outputs included under-editing and occasional damage to critical items or technical names.
26B MoE.recommendation: Use only when review on the exact workload proves a quality gain worth the latency.

response_format_ab.manipulation: plain output versus schema-constrained JSON output
response_format_ab.constant_input_representation: plain full context and the same current chunk
response_format_ab.warning: Do not cite this as plain-context versus JSON-context evidence.

long_context_representation.timestamps: not default; highest overhead and the only observed 12B cross-boundary continuation
long_context_representation.json_input: identity-friendly but no stable quality advantage; representation-specific deletion exists for E4B

structured_output.runtime_schema: compact generic items array with id and text
application_validation: raw parse or explicitly logged single-fence recovery
exact item count, ID set, uniqueness, and order
chunk boundaries, protected values, omissions, additions, and repetition

concurrency.bounded_8k: P2 passed; repaired P4 passed structurally
concurrency.full_approximately_23k: P2 failed 0/4 before generation; use P1 sequential in the tested runtime
concurrency.principle: Concurrency admission is workload- and allocation-specific, not a global model property.

observed: sequential capacity recovery after pre-generation HTTP 400
mechanical 24-item block merge with exact IDs
not_observed: retry after generated malformed JSON or schema/ID defects

cross_cutting.cache: Lifecycle reuse and hints exist; physical KV reuse and avoided prefill remain unproven.
cross_cutting.gpu: Automatic placement remains default; maximum-GPU mode showed no proven benefit without layer-aware, output-normalized telemetry.

12b_block_merge.verdict: structural and semantic pass for conservative lossless use; cleanup needs improvement

remaining_gaps: Active application persistence and fallback path.
Controlled capacity change only if full-prefix P2 is operationally required.
Physical cache and per-layer GPU telemetry.
